patterns.markdownDescription:
Date-time formats for parsing timestamps from input events

Lists date-time formats in [Java specification](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/format/DateTimeFormatter.html) for parsing timestamp epoch values from input events. (Default: ["[config.yaml](https://github.com/log-10x/config/tree/main/pipelines/run/config/transform/timestamp/config.yaml)"])

searchDirection.markdownDescription:
Controls the text direction(s) in which to search for timestamps

Controls the direction(s) to search for timestamps within an TenXObject's [text](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text) field. Possible values:   - **fromStart**: search for [timestampMaxPerObject](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampmaxperobject) from the start of [text]https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text) to restrict the search to [searchLengthLimitFromStart](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampsearchlengthlimitfromstart) characters    - **fromEnd**: search backward for [timestampMaxPerObject](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampmaxperobject) from the end of [text](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text) to limit the search to [searchLengthLimitFromEnd](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampsearchlengthlimitfromend) characters    - **fromStartAndEnd** search for [timestampMaxPerObject](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampmaxperobject) from both the start and end of [text](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text) to limit the search to [searchLengthLimitFromStart](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampsearchlengthlimitfromstart) and  [searchLengthLimitFromEnd](https://doc.log10x.com/run/transform/timestamp/#timestampsearchlengthlimitfromend) respectively    - **none**: do not parse timestamps (Default: fromStart)

maxPerObject.markdownDescription:
Maximum number of timestamps per TenXObject

Controls the maximum number of timestamps to add into an TenXObject's [timestamp](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXObject+timestamp) array. Set to 0 for unlimited. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 0)

searchLengthLimitFromStart.markdownDescription:
Maximum number of characters to scan for timestamps from event start

Limits the number of characters to search for timestamps from the beginning of the object's [text](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text)  field. Set 0 for unlimited. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 0)

searchLengthLimitFromEnd.markdownDescription:
Max number of characters to scan for timestamps from event end

Limits the number of characters to search for timestamps from the end of the object's [text](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text) field. Set 0 for unlimited. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 0)

zone.markdownDescription:
Timezone to use when formatting timestamp Unix epoch values

Controls the timezone used when formatting timestamp epoch string values. The [ZoneId.of](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/ZoneId.html#of-java.lang.String-)  obtains the timezone from the 'zone' value. (Default: [host OS timezone](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/ZoneId.html#systemDefault--))

literals.markdownDescription:
Timestamp literals

An array of strings literal values to treat as valid parts of timestamp (e.g., 'Z', 'T', 'GMT'). (Default: ["T","Z"])
